t_ch: 你是说……
t_en: Do you mean...

t_ch: 我可能…我大概不需要知道其中细节。我听说过一些和无名客有关的故事，我猜你也一定和传闻中那些人一样本领滔天。
t_en: I... I probably don't need to know the details. I've heard stories about the Nameless, and I believe you must be as talented as those in the stories.

t_ch: 还记得螺丝咕姆吗？
t_en: Do you remember Screwllum?

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆…？螺丝……
t_en: Screwllum? Screwy...

t_ch: 我是来帮你的，查德威克博士。
t_en: I'm here to help you, Dr. Chadwick.

t_ch: 帮我…啊，这件事我大概还记得。我必须承认…我没抱太大希望。那对我来说太奢侈了。
t_en: Help me... Oh, I still remember something about this. I must confess... that I didn't get my hopes up. Hope is a luxury for me.

t_ch: 还记得螺丝咕姆吗？
t_en: Do you remember Screwllum?

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆…？螺丝……
t_en: Screwllum? Screwy...

t_ch: 像是被闪电击中一般，查德威克的瞳孔突然放大了一倍——然后又迅速地收缩。笼罩他眼白的灰雾开始散去，他神态中的一切都变得明朗起来。
t_en: As if struck by lightning, Chadwick's eyes suddenly widen and quickly contract again. The gloom covering his eyes dissipates, and he appears bright-eyed.

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆…他在哪儿？他答应过我…他说过他会帮助……
t_en: Screwllum... Where's he? He promised... he would help me...

t_ch: 不…不对。他的确履行了承诺，他没有辜负我的信任。消失的人是我…我的耳朵和嘴巴都被堵住了，眼睛也被蒙上……
t_en: Wait... No. He did fulfill his promise, and he didn't fail me. I was the one who went missing... Both my ears and mouth were covered, and I was blindfolded...

t_ch: 为什么？一切都那么混乱，那么无序。我是「天才俱乐部」的一员，这我记得起来…但我做了什么？我又忘记了什么？是谁把我束缚在了这幻象里……
t_en: Why? Why is everything in such a chaotic, disorderly manner? I remember I'm a member of the Genius Society... But what did I do, and what have I forgotten? Who bound me to this illusionary world...

t_ch: 向查德威克展示那瓶药丸。
t_en: Show the bottle of pills to Chadwick.

t_ch: 这是…药丸？
t_en: Is this a bottle of... pills?

t_ch: 它能帮你回忆起来。
t_en: It can help you recover your memory.

t_ch: 我明白了…这是螺丝想到的办法，对吗？
t_en: Got it... This is Screw's idea, right?

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆想要帮你。
t_en: Screwllum wants to help you.

t_ch: 我明白了…这是他想到的办法，对吗？
t_en: Got it... This is his idea, right?

t_ch: 现在，我的胸腔里像是装着整个宇宙。我能看到它的每次闪烁，感受到它的每次跃动——但实际上，我对它一无所知。
t_en: Now, my chest feels like it holds the entire universe in it. I can see it flicker and feel its pulse, but I know nothing about it.

t_ch: 但在刚才，你拿出那个药瓶的时候，从那片宇宙的深远角落传来了一个声音。那个声音…它想说服我相信螺丝咕姆和你。
t_en: But when you showed me the bottle of pills earlier, a voice came from a distant corner of my universe. It... tried to persuade me to trust Screwllum and you.

t_ch: 查德威克露出一个笑容，你第一次从他的脸上读到由衷的喜悦。
t_en: Chadwick's face beams with a smile. It is the first time you have seen genuine joy on his face.

t_ch: 现在的我，还有什么资格挑三拣四呢？把它给我吧，孩子。
t_en: Who am I to be picky now, right? I'll take it.

t_ch: 我来帮你。
t_en: Let me help.

t_ch: 再等我一会儿。
t_en: Just a moment, please.

t_ch: …出了什么状况吗？
t_en: ...Is there a problem?

t_ch: 别担心，我哪也不去…我哪也去不了。我在这里等你回来，孩子。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm not going anywhere, because I can't. I'll wait here for your return.

t_ch: 我准备好了，孩子。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 年轻的卡尔德隆•查德威克就站在你的面前。几个琥珀纪过后，你仍然一眼便能看出他神态中的积虑。
t_en: The young Calderon Chadwick is standing right before you. He wears the same contemplative expression even after several Amber Eras.

t_ch: 就是这里吗？
t_en: Is this the place?

t_ch: 没错，查德威克博士。以这里作为起点，我们——{RUBY_B#公司部门}技术研发部{RUBY_E#}和你的团队——将共同开创银河的未来。
t_en: Yes, Dr. Chadwick. Starting from here, we, the {RUBY_B#IPC department}Technology Department{RUBY_E#}, and your team will shape the future of the galaxy together.

t_ch: 话可别说太满了，杜德利。对于这个项目，我还不敢说有百分百的把握。
t_en: Don't be overconfident, Dudley. I'm not fully certain that this project will work out.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…放心吧，我们需要的不是<unbreak>100%</unbreak>的成功率——我们需要的是有你在我们的团队里。
t_en: Hahaha... Take it easy. What we need is you on our team, not a <unbreak>100%</unbreak> success rate.

t_ch: 说真的，杜…我有些担心。你们想要的东西，那种只存在于理论构想里的「武器」…它真的有可能颠覆银河。
t_en: Honestly speaking, Dud, I'm a bit apprehensive about this. What you guys are seeking is a weapon that only exists theoretically... It could cause an upheaval in the galaxy.

t_ch: …当然了，查。可别低估了技术研发部，我们或许没有你的天才头脑，但绝对不缺「远见」。
t_en: ...Of course, Chad. Don't ever underestimate the Technology Department. We might not be geniuses like you, but we're plenty "visionary."

t_ch: {RUBY_B#存护}琥珀王{RUBY_E#}刚刚挥锤，宣告了一个新世纪的开始，也代表传说中的寰宇巨灾愈发迫近……
t_en: The {RUBY_B#Preservation}Amber Lord{RUBY_E#} just swung their hammer, announcing the beginning of a new era. It means that the legendary cosmic catastrophe is drawing close...

t_ch: 倒是不必给我科普这些，我读过《星空简史》…不过真没想到，你居然也是「灾难说」的支持者。
t_en: No explanation needed. I've read The Brief History of the Universe to know enough... By the way, I'm surprised you're a supporter of the catastrophe theory.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，那倒不一定。我支持更具商业挖掘潜质的理论，仅此而已。
t_en: Hahaha, that's not totally true. I'll just support any theory with the most commercial value.

t_ch: …像是你能说出来的话。
t_en: ...It sounds like something you would say.

t_ch: 咳咳，但无论如何你都不能否认：自从「万界之癌」开始散布，诸如军团和泯灭帮之流的活动变得愈发活跃了。我们在这里的工作就是为对抗那些威胁做准备。
t_en: Ahem, there's something you can't deny: Since the Cancer of All Worlds started spreading, the Legion and the Annihilation Gang have become more and more active. Our mission here is to prepare and ward off those threats.

t_ch: ——我不想说什么「我们的事业是正义的」之类的话…我知道你没那么天真，查。我只是觉得，人有些时候可以把事情想得单纯些…那样日子就能好过很多。
t_en: I don't want to say things like: "We're working for a just cause," because I know you aren't that naïve, Chad. I just think that our lives can be a lot easier... if we don't complicate stuff.

t_ch: 我可不想在这地方和你扯上一整天。我们下去吧。
t_en: I don't want to spend all day delving into this subject with you. Let's go down.

t_ch: 那些东西是…真蛰虫？！
t_en: Are those... True Stings!?

t_ch: 没错，恐怕是寰宇蝗灾时期留下来的。已经太久无人问津这颗星球了，在这座设施里看到什么我都不意外。
t_en: You're right, and I think they're probably from the Swarm Disaster era. It's been a long while since anyone stepped foot here. I won't be surprised by anything I see in this facility.

t_ch: …亏你们能找到这么个地方。我还是第一次看到活体。
t_en: ...I'm impressed you guys managed to find this place. This is the first time I've seen living True Stings.

t_ch: 哈，可惜你不是生物学家。先把这里清理干净吧，要不然可没法开工。
t_en: It's a pity you aren't a biologist. Let's clear this place up, or we can't start working.

t_ch: …这扇门之后，女士们先生们，就是我们烹制「奇迹」的后厨。
t_en: ...Ladies and gentlemen, the space beyond this door is where we concoct the "miracle."

t_ch: 查德威克博士就在里面吗？
t_en: Is Dr. Chadwick inside?

t_ch: 没想到有一天能和那位「天才」共事……
t_en: I never thought I'd end up working alongside that genius...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，别着急。在诸位和博士成为正式战友之前，我还有些话要说。
t_en: Hahaha, please hold your horses. Before you all officially become Dr. Chadwick's colleagues, there's something I want to remind you.

t_ch: 跨过这道门，就意味着各位选择了自己的第二次人生。从今往后的很长一段时间里，这座实验室就会是你们生活的全部。
t_en: By walking through this door, you'll be selecting a different life for yourselves. This lab will be your life for a very long time to come.

t_ch: 你们会在这里工作、研究、娱乐、谈心；你们会与世隔绝，直到项目问世或宣告失败。
t_en: You will work, research, have fun, and make friends here, isolated from the outside world until this project is launched or fails.

t_ch: 如果你们还没有准备好迎接这样的生活——趁着大门还没打开，离开还来得及。
t_en: If you aren't ready for this kind of life, you may go before I open this door.

t_ch: …我从十五岁开始就在研究虚数理论，能和查德威克博士共事是我毕生的愿望。
t_en: ...I've been studying Imaginary theories since the age of <unbreak>15</unbreak>. Working with Dr. Chadwick is my lifelong ambition.

t_ch: 对…这是其他人毕生难求的机会。一扇门算什么？我们是科学家，我们早就把一生献给了科学。
t_en: Right... This is an opportunity others could only dream of. That door is nothing! As scientists, we've long dedicated our lives to science.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…像，你们和他真的很像。我相信各位与博士共事的时光一定会十分愉快。
t_en: Hahaha... You guys are just like him! I believe you will have a great time working with Dr. Chadwick.

t_ch: 那么，女士们先生们，请进——
t_en: Well, ladies and gentlemen, please come on in...

t_ch: …第三十七次试爆实验，失败。
t_en: ...The <unbreak>37</unbreak>th detonation test, unsuccessful.

t_ch: 对撞产生的能量波没能成功撕开虚数裂口；引爆胶囊未能达到临界状态。
t_en: The energy waves from the collision failed to tear up the Imaginary fissure. The detonation capsule did not reach the critical threshold.

t_ch: 到底是为什么……
t_en: Why...

t_ch: …解除安全协议吧。将对撞速率提高到默认阈值的<unbreak>1.3</unbreak>倍——我们从这里开始。
t_en: ...Lift the safety protocols. Increase the collision rate to <unbreak>1.3</unbreak> times the default threshold. Let's take it from there.

t_ch: 咦？！但、但是……
t_en: Eh!? B—But...

t_ch: 这种行为太冒险了…甚至可能会危害到实验人员的安全。
t_en: This is too risky... and might even compromise the researchers' safety.

t_ch: 是啊！我们可以再试一次，没必要……
t_en: Exactly! We can try one more time. There's no need to...

t_ch: …我们已经试过太多次了。很明显，按照原样走下去也不会有进展。
t_en: ...We've tried too many times. It's clear that we won't make any progress if we go down the same road.

t_ch: 过去的三年，我们翻过了几乎所有的理论障碍。在这个秘密实验室里诞生的科研成果足够震撼整个宇宙。
t_en: Over the past three years, we've overcome nearly every theoretical hurdle. The scientific achievements born in this lab are enough to leave the universe amazed.

t_ch: 但如果我们无法成功造出「虚数坍缩脉冲」…所有努力都会付之东流。公司不会允许我们把任何成果带出这里，连一页纸都不可能。
t_en: But if we fail to build the Imaginary Implosion Pulse... our efforts will all be in vain. The IPC won't allow us to take the scientific results with us, not even a single page.

t_ch: 作为物理学家，我们别无选择，唯有成功。
t_en: As physicists, we must succeed.

t_ch: …您能亲自前来观看试爆，我和我的团队倍感荣幸。这个实验室平时绝不会接待客人…幸好螺丝咕姆先生您的身份特殊，技术研发部不便推辞。
t_en: ...My team and I are honored to have you here to watch the detonation test. This lab doesn't welcome visitors, but you're special, Mr. Screwllum. The Technology Department wants to stay in your good graces.

t_ch: 不必感谢我，博士。您和您的同僚在此取得的成果令我深感震撼。
t_en: You're welcome, Dr. Chadwick. I'm floored by you and your colleagues' achievements.

t_ch: 但请允许我提出疑问——和有机生命相同，好奇心有时会驱使我打破礼仪的界限——您为什么会同意参与研发「虚数坍缩脉冲」这种武器？
t_en: Please allow me to ask you some questions, as curiosity sometimes compels me to ignore the decorum, just like what organic life experiences. Why did you agree to take part in the development of a weapon as destructive as the Imaginary Implosion Pulse?

t_ch: 因为杜德利…我在技术研发部的朋友，他说过——这件武器能被用来打击「毁灭」的势力。我们在做的是正确的事。
t_en: Dudley, my friend from the Technology Department, said that the weapon could be used to contain the Destruction's forces. He said we were doing the right thing.

t_ch: 非常抱歉，查德威克博士。我不应如此唐突地提问，导致您不得不给出违心的答案。
t_en: I'm very sorry, Dr. Chadwick. I shouldn't have sprung that question on you and put you in a position to give an insincere answer.

t_ch: 我想，我们每个人都有权选择自己生活的方式。我们可以放弃那些会令自身迷惘的「冗余思考」，只把视线投向自己身前之物，和自己擅长的领域。
t_en: I think we're all free to choose the life we prefer. We can put aside those redundant thoughts that distract us and focus on our tasks at hand and areas of expertise.

t_ch: 但要小心，博士。对于你我这类人，一时的懈驰可能导向万千生灵的哀恸。智慧是宇宙对我们的赐福，也是它强加于我们的重负。
t_en: But you must be cautious, Doctor. For people like us, even a minor slipup can impact the entire universe. Our wisdom is both a blessing and a burden.

t_ch: 有些话语现在传达已经太晚。如今我们已经知晓，魔盒里的确装着一只怪物。但愿您能守住它，不让它被无知的孩子随手打开。
t_en: Some advice is too late to be given now. Now, we already know that the box contains a monster. I hope you'll safeguard it and make sure it won't be opened by some ignorant kids.

t_ch: …谢谢您的忠告，螺丝先生。希望有朝一日我们还能再见。
t_en: ...Thanks for your advice, Mr. Screwllum. I hope I'll see you again one day.

t_ch: …为什么，杜德利？！我们不止一次，推心置腹地谈过这件事…我每天都在警告你们！滥用它可能导致的后果，你心知肚明！
t_en: ...Why, Dudley!? We've talked about this more than once... I've been warning you guys every single day! You know very well what could happen if it is misused!

t_ch: 这里有很多跟你我一样的普通人，他们把自己四分之一的人生都贡献给了这项事业，有些还为它付出了惨痛的代价——他们…我们想看到的不是这个结果！
t_en: Many of our colleagues here are ordinary folks just like us. They have dedicated a quarter of their lives to this project, and some have even paid a hefty price for it. They... This isn't how we wanted things to end up!

t_ch: …很遗憾，查。你在自己的领域是「天才」，但却对权力的结构一无所知。你过于天真…所以才会看不清大局，甚至对于自己身处什么样的时代一无所知。
t_en: ...I'm very sorry to tell you this, Chad. You might be a genius in your field, but you know nothing about power. Your naivety blinds you from seeing the big picture, and you don't even understand the era you're in.

t_ch: 你早该明白的，你我在这场局里都只是棋子。棋子一旦落到棋盘上，剩下的就只有身不由己了。
t_en: You should have realized long ago that we're both merely pawns in this game of chess. Once we're placed on the chessboard, we are no longer in control of our fate.

t_ch: 「武器」被制造出来就是为了打击敌人，这道理很难理解吗？十余支军团的先遣队正在准备袭击联合商路，他们的存在可能直接威胁到庇尔波因特。
t_en: Weapons are made to eliminate enemies. Is it that hard to understand? Over a dozen vanguard units of the Legion are preparing to attack the joint trade routes. Their presence can directly threaten Pier Point's safety.

t_ch: 敌人近在眼前——眼下就是检验「虚数坍缩脉冲」威力的最佳时机！
t_en: Our enemies are closing in... This is the perfect time to test the power of the Imaginary Implosion Pulse!

t_ch: 一百种…不，你们有一千种办法阻止他们，还能付出更小的代价、波及更少的无辜者。
t_en: You have a hundred... no, a thousand other ways to stop them that can incur lower costs and affect fewer innocents.

t_ch: 我终于理解了…过去的二十年，在这里发生的一切都只是一场实验。我们亲手建造的一切…却从来就不属于我们。
t_en: I've got it, finally. What we've done here for the past twenty years is just an experiment. The result of our work... never truly belongs to us.

t_ch: 别骗自己了，查——你打一开始就知道自己趟的是哪滩浑水。你只是选择性地视而不见而已。
t_en: Stop lying to yourself, Chad. You knew what you were getting into right from the beginning, but you chose to ignore it.

t_ch: 过来吧，「起爆专家」。该让军团尝尝我们的厉害了。
t_en: Come here, "bomb-maker." It's time to show the Legion our might.

t_ch: 求求你…求求你。
t_en: Please... I'm begging you.

t_ch: 不能这样…不该这样的。无数人会因我丧命……
t_en: We can't do this.... We shouldn't do this. Countless lives will be lost because of me...

t_ch: …弹道轨迹预计算已经完成。命中误差区间：±<unbreak>0.08%</unbreak>。
t_en: ...Ballistic trajectory precalculation is complete. Margin of error: ±<unbreak>0.08%</unbreak>.

t_ch: 起爆台充能准备中…<unbreak>60%</unbreak>…<unbreak>70%</unbreak>…<unbreak>85%</unbreak>…完成。
t_en: Charging detonation platform... <unbreak>60%</unbreak>... <unbreak>70%</unbreak>... <unbreak>85%</unbreak>... Complete.

t_ch: 检查对撞起爆器状态…<unbreak>30%</unbreak>…<unbreak>50%</unbreak>…<unbreak>75%</unbreak>…完成。
t_en: Reviewing collider status... <unbreak>30%</unbreak>... <unbreak>50%</unbreak>... <unbreak>75%</unbreak>... Complete.

t_ch: 自动巡航AI已部署。触敌引爆AI已部署。
t_en: Automatic cruise AI deployed. Enemy contact detonation AI deployed.

t_ch: 「虚数坍缩脉冲」启动准备：已完成。随时可以起爆。
t_en: Imaginary Implosion Pulse preparation: Complete. Ready to detonate at any moment.

t_ch: …爆炸范围内有多少非敌方目标？
t_en: ...How many collateral targets are there in the blast range?

t_ch: 正在计算……
t_en: Calculation in progress...

t_ch: 预计起爆范围内共有<unbreak>24</unbreak>颗卫星级及以上星体。其中包含3颗存在D级以上级别文明的次级行星。
t_en: There are an estimated <unbreak>24</unbreak> planets at the satellite level or above within the blast range. Among them, there are three secondary planets with civilizations of Grade D or above.

t_ch: 嗯…连带损伤在可接受范围内。起爆计划照常。
t_en: Hmm... the amount of collateral damage is acceptable. The detonation plan can proceed as scheduled.

t_ch: 时候到了，专家。按下起爆按钮吧。
t_en: It's time, bomb-maker. Hit the detonate button.

t_ch: 不按起爆按钮。
t_en: Do not press the button.

t_ch: …这是什么意思？我说了——按下起爆按钮！
t_en: ...What are you doing? Press it now!

t_ch: 啊…我明白了。你害怕背上骂名，对吧？
t_en: Ah... I get it. You're afraid of taking the blame, aren't you?

t_ch: 不用担心。按下那个钮的不是你，也不是我；后人的史书上根本不会提到我们两个的名字。
t_en: Fret not. The one who presses the button is neither you nor I. We won't even be mentioned if this incident is later reported.

t_ch: 现在…按下它吧。
t_en: Now... Do it.

t_ch: 不按起爆按钮。
t_en: Do not press the button.

t_ch: …你同情查德威克博士，是吗？
t_en: ...You sympathize with Dr. Chadwick, right?

t_ch: 真遗憾，我本不想戳破你的偶像气泡…但卡尔德隆•查德威克不值得你的同情。他早就预见到了近似的结局，但还是选择了忘记后果，不惜代价投入研究。
t_en: I'm sorry to burst your bubble, but Calderon Chadwick isn't worth your sympathy. He'd long foreseen the outcome, but he chose to ignore it and carry on the research at all costs.

t_ch: 他的自私就是把自己的才能置于一切之前——其他人的事？他压根就不在乎。
t_en: Selfishly, he puts his talent before everything else. He doesn't care the least bit about anyone else.

t_ch: 现在…按下它吧。
t_en: Now... Do it.

t_ch: 不按起爆按钮。
t_en: Do not press the button.

t_ch: …我不想再浪费口舌了，你刚刚浪费了最后一次机会。
t_en: ...I'm not going to say anything else. You've just wasted your final opportunity.

t_ch: 士兵——动手。
t_en: Hey, you. Do it.

t_ch: 按下起爆按钮。
t_en: Hit the detonate button.

t_ch: 按下起爆按钮。
t_en: Hit the detonate button.

t_ch: 按下起爆按钮。
t_en: Hit the detonate button.

t_ch: 起爆指令已确认。对撞起爆器开始充能…<unbreak>20%</unbreak>…<unbreak>50%</unbreak>…<unbreak>75%</unbreak>…已达到起爆临界点。
t_en: Detonation command confirmed. Collider charging in progress... <unbreak>20%</unbreak>... <unbreak>50%</unbreak>... <unbreak>75%</unbreak>... Critical detonation threshold reached.

t_ch: 自动巡航AI已开启。锁定目标中…<unbreak>30%</unbreak>…<unbreak>70%</unbreak>…完成。
t_en: Automatic cruise AI activated. Locking target... <unbreak>30%</unbreak>... <unbreak>70%</unbreak>... Complete.

t_ch: 锁定AI已开启。升温中…<unbreak>20%</unbreak>…<unbreak>40%</unbreak>…<unbreak>80%</unbreak>…腔室温度已达到阈值。
t_en: Lock-on AI activated. Warming up... <unbreak>20%</unbreak>... <unbreak>40%</unbreak>... <unbreak>80%</unbreak>... Chamber temperature has reached threshold.

t_ch: 「虚数坍缩脉冲」即将引爆。<size=28>五……</size>
t_en: Imaginary Implosion Pulse is about to be launched. <size=28>Five...</size>

t_ch: 杜，我……
t_en: Dud, I...

t_ch: <size=28>四……</size>
t_en: <size=28>Four...</size>

t_ch: …不要怪我，查。我们都要为自己的选择负责。
t_en: ...Don't hold it against me, Chad. We have to be responsible for our choices.

t_ch: <size=28>三……</size>
t_en: <size=28>Three...</size>

t_ch: 他们会永世诅咒我的名字，他们会…你又付出了什么代价，杜德利？
t_en: My name will forever be cursed. They'll... What's the price you have to pay, Dudley?

t_ch: <size=28>二……</size>
t_en: <size=28>Two...</size>

t_ch: 看吧，查…这就是你的问题，你总把自己的天赋和才能视作理所应当。你完全不知道还有一种人，像我这样的人——
t_en: Look, Chad... This is your problem. You always take your gifts and talents for granted, and you have no idea there are people like me...

t_ch: <size=28>一……</size>
t_en: <size=28>One...</size>

t_ch: ——我们不会被任何人记得。
t_en: ...No one will remember us.

t_ch: <size=28>零。</size>
t_en: <size=28>Zero...</size>

t_ch: <size=28>「虚数坍缩脉冲」——引爆。</size>
t_en: <size=28>Imaginary Implosion Pulse — detonate.</size>

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}…又见面了。
t_en: Oh, {NICKNAME}. It's you again.

t_ch: 现在…你经历了我一半的人生，也知道我的故事了。
t_en: Now, you have experienced half of my life and learned about my story.

t_ch: 虚数脉冲杀死了多少人？
t_en: How many people did the Imaginary Pulse kill?

t_ch: …一个我竭尽全力想要忘掉的数字。那天以后，我再也不敢向镜子投去哪怕一瞥。我害怕看到一个恶魔…一个夺走无数人生命和家园的凶手。
t_en: ...A number that I try my best every day to forget. Ever since that day, I haven't dared to look in the mirror even once. I'm worried I might see a monster... A murderer who has killed countless people and ruined even more lives.

t_ch: 我不知道该怎么看待你。
t_en: I'm not sure what to make of you.

t_ch: 我也一样，孩子…那天以后，我再也不敢向镜子投去哪怕一瞥。
t_en: Me too, child... Ever since that day, I don't dare to even glance at myself in the mirror.

t_ch: 我害怕看到一个恶魔…一个夺走无数人生命和家园的凶手。
t_en: I'm worried I might see a monster... A murderer who has killed countless people and ruined even more lives.

t_ch: 那天在控制室里也是我最后一次看到杜德利。或许现实就和他说的一样，棋子是不配拥有名字的。
t_en: That day in the control room was the last I saw Dudley. Perhaps, it's true that pawns don't deserve to be mentioned, just like how he put it.

t_ch: 再之后，我遣散了团队，废弃了原本的实验室。我想办法带走了一些关键的资料，不希望它们落入别有用心之人手中。
t_en: After that, I disbanded the team and left the lab. I managed to take some crucial data with me to keep them from falling into the wrong hands.

t_ch: 我从他人口中得知，下令使用「虚数坍缩脉冲」的技术研发部高层被部门专门立项审查，后来失去了音信。
t_en: I later learned from others that the higher-ups of the Technology Department who ordered the Imaginary Implosion Pulse detonation were subjected to an internal review. They were never heard of again.

t_ch: 但我清楚公司不会善罢甘休。我们的研究成果太过宝贵…哪怕他们明面上发表声明谴责，也不可能真的放弃和虚数脉冲有关的技术。
t_en: But I knew that IPC wouldn't let the matter rest. The result of our research was way too valuable... Although they released a statement condemning the detonation, they would never really give up the technology related to it.

t_ch: 他们需要我——为了掌控那些知识，也为了不让它们落入银河间其它派系之手。他们会追我到天涯海角。
t_en: They needed me to control the knowledge and keep it out of reach of other factions across the universe. So, they would always be breathing down my neck.

t_ch: …所以我开始了逃亡，我不想穷尽一生都活在一片阴影之下。
t_en: ...So, I decided to escape, not wanting to live the rest of my life being controlled by others.

t_ch: 在我最需要帮助的时候，我只想到了一个名字。我们此前只有一面之缘，但他却戳破了我的虚伪和借口。
t_en: A name came to my mind when I needed help the most. We only met once, but he saw through my hypocrisy and excuses.

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆。
t_en: Screwllum.

t_ch: 对。我下定决心找到他，相信他能帮我反抗公司。
t_en: Yes. I made up my mind to look for him, thinking that he could help me rebel against IPC.

t_ch: 这就是我后半生的故事……
t_en: This is the story of the latter part of my life...

t_ch: 曾几何时，在世界与世界互相隔绝的年代，某些文明已经拥有了足够毁灭自身的武器。
t_en: Once upon a time when there was no interaction between different worlds, some civilizations were already in possession of weapons powerful enough to destroy themselves.

t_ch: 对力量的畏惧让他们建立起了复杂的规则和律法，极力避免自毁的开关落入疯子或暴徒之手。
t_en: Out of fear for power, they established complicated laws and regulations in an attempt to prevent these self-unmaking devices from falling into the hands of the insane or the violent.

t_ch: 后来，当星海被曲折蜿蜒的航路连成一片，技术和资源开始在世界之间流动，比原本那些武器强大数万倍的新武器也逐一问世。
t_en: When the starry seas later became interconnected by winding routes, the exchange of technology and resources began. New weapons, many times more powerful than the original ones, emerged one after another.

t_ch: 令人不解的是：随着武器威力的擢升，那些原本被用来限制滥用的繁杂规则却被愈发简化。当刀尖的方向从向内变成向外，人们对暴力的态度不约而同地变得宽容。
t_en: Bewilderingly, those complicated rules in place to limit the abuse of weapons became increasingly simplified as more and more powerful weapons were invented. People were more tolerant of weapons when they were used against enemies.

t_ch: 假如在某个遥远的世界上，万千生灵正被烈火摧残，悲鸣响彻云霄——但对于在树下仰望夜空的你我而言，那不过是漫天繁星被谁人摘下了一颗而已。
t_en: Let's say there was a faraway planet where its people were being ravaged by ferocious flames with their cries echoing throughout the sky. Yet, for you and I, lying beneath the tree and gazing at the sky, the planet would be just another star plucked by someone from many others.

t_ch: 「智慧」，多么令人迷惑的词汇。有人以为它是一切问题的答案，一切美德的终点。
t_en: "Wisdom." What a perplexing word. Some see it as the answer to every problem and the ultimate of all virtues.

t_ch: 但银河间多少智识超凡之人，却受尽世俗烦扰。究其原因，到底是天才太过高傲，还是庸人过分嫉妒？没人说得清楚。
t_en: However, many of those with extraordinary intellect are troubled by secular problems. Is it because the geniuses are too arrogant, or is it because jealousy gets the better of the mundane? No one has an answer for that.

t_ch: 唯有一点无须争辩：智慧可以与自私的欲念共存。
t_en: Only one thing is irrefutable: Wisdom can co-exist with selfish desires.

t_ch: 当赞达尔用古老且陌生的语言将「虚数之树」几个字书于纸上…他的眼中看到了什么？
t_en: What did Zandar see when he was recording tomes on the "Imaginary Tree" in his strange and ancient language?

t_ch: 当我还是一个孩童，第一次接触虚数理论时，在我脑海中浮现的画面不是一棵参天大树；而是一片茂密的树海。
t_en: When I was little and first discovered the Imaginary theories, what came to my mind was the image of a dense forest, not of a huge tree.

t_ch: 我所见过的一切，令我全然无法相信宇宙的基底是一棵无私的巨树，自愿为亿万枚树叶奉献、给养。
t_en: After going through so many things, I still find it hard to believe that the foundation of the universe is a giant tree that nurtures countless leaves selflessly and voluntarily.

t_ch: 我宁愿相信，每个世界都是一枚随风飘来的树种，因为巧合落在了这片土地上，然后开始自顾自地扎根、生长，最后形成了这片树海。
t_en: I prefer to see each planet as a seed carried by the wind that happens to land on the soil. The seeds then begin to take root and grow bigger, eventually forming the forest.

t_ch: 宇宙并不在意我们——宇宙只是存在着，并凝望着。
t_en: The universe isn't concerned with our presence... It merely exists and observes.

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆…终于，我终于找到你了。
t_en: Screwllum... I've finally found you.

t_ch: 我们很多年没见了，查德威克博士。请原谅，我无法由衷地对您道出「我很遗憾」——因为我知道你早就预见了这个结果。
t_en: We haven't seen each other for years, Dr. Chadwick. Please forgive me for not telling you that I feel sorry for your plight. I know you have long seen it coming.

t_ch: 我经历的一切都是自己种出的结果…这么多年过去，我已经不需要再被人提醒这一点了。
t_en: I brought this upon myself... After all these years, I no longer have to be reminded of that.

t_ch: 我现在需要帮助，螺丝先生……
t_en: I need help, Mr. Screwllum...

t_ch: 你历尽劫难后仍要和我对话…你一定是无路可走了。如有需要，螺丝星可以成为你暂时的庇护所。
t_en: I can tell you're driven to a corner, as you're still willing to talk to me after going through all those hardships. Planet Screwllum can be your temporary shelter if you need it.

t_ch: 不，不行。我已经对自己发过誓，绝不会再把无关的人卷进来了。
t_en: No. No way. I've pledged to myself never to involve any more innocent people in this matter.

t_ch: 不管我躲到银河的哪个角落，公司都会找到我的…这是早晚的事。我不可能一直逃下去，但我还有别的方式反抗。
t_en: No matter where I hide, the IPC will find me. It's just a matter of time. I can't be on the run forever, so I need to fight back in some way.

t_ch: 我听说了，俱乐部里有一位天才研制出了一种秘密药物，可以帮人「屏蔽」部分意识和记忆。但我和俱乐部的各位向来交情不深，所以才想到找你帮忙牵线。
t_en: I've heard that a genius has developed a secret drug capable of blocking parts of the consciousness and memory of a person. I've never been close with anyone from the Genius Society, so I'd need you to pull the strings for me.

t_ch: 「选择性阻断药」——我有所耳闻，大概是那位脾气古怪的药剂师的发明。的确，那位女士从不与公司来往，不必担心她会做出出卖同僚的行为。
t_en: The Selective Blocking Agent — I've heard of it. I suppose it's the brainchild of that eccentric pharmacist. Indeed, that lady has no dealings with the IPC, so the risk of her selling you out is low.

t_ch: 疑问：你是否认定这是最稳妥的解决方案？
t_en: Question: Do you think this is a surefire solution?

t_ch: …这样代价最小，我确定。只要我能将那些知识暂时遗忘…公司就什么也别想从我的脑子里榨出来。
t_en: ...All I'm certain is that it has the smallest price to pay. As long as I can forget the knowledge temporarily... The IPC will have no means to extract it from my brain.

t_ch: 至于我所要经受的任何事…那都是我应得的。人总要为自己的行为负责，承担代价也是必要的一环……
t_en: As for whatever I'll be going through, I deserve it. Everyone must be accountable for their actions, and facing the consequences is part and parcel of the process...

t_ch: 我理解了，查德威克博士。我会协助你拿到那样药物。
t_en: I understand, Dr. Chadwick. I'll get you that drug.

t_ch: 你终于能够诚实地面对自己了，博士——仅就这点，我为你感到欣慰。我衷心希望你能挺过这段艰难的时光。
t_en: You're finally honest with yourself, doctor. I'm glad for you for that achievement alone. I hope with all my heart that you can pull through these tough times.

t_ch: 眼前的查德威克博士大概已经年过古稀——他的形象与你们初遇时相比已经几无差别。
t_en: The Dr. Chadwick in front of you is at an advanced age. He looks very similar to how he was when you first met him.

t_ch: 依然是那双积虑的眼睛，眼白里又多夹杂了几分浑浊。
t_en: He still has those contemplative eyes, now tinged with a hint of gloominess.

t_ch: 他似乎感受不到你的存在…或许你该先在四周转转，观察一下这个房间。
t_en: He can't seem to sense your presence... Perhaps, you can take a look around the room first.

t_ch: 博士？
t_en: Doctor?

t_ch: 你能感受到我吗？
t_en: Can you feel me?

t_ch: 啊…他们又派了新人来，是吗？
t_en: Ah... Did they send their guys over again?

t_ch: 换谁来都是一样的。同样的话我已经说过无数次…我现在对你们而言百无一用。
t_en: Who is sent here makes no difference. I've said time and again that I'm no longer of use to you guys.

t_ch: 你用了「选择性阻断药」…
t_en: You took the Selective Blocking Agent...

t_ch: 你…怎么会知道？
t_en: How... did you know?

t_ch: 啊…我明白了。这里不是现实…你也不是公司派来的人，对吗？
t_en: Ah... I get it. This isn't reality, and you aren't an IPC lackey, are you?

t_ch: 太好了…我已经太久没和人正常地对过话了。我在这里…什么都不缺，他们会满足我的任何要求…我只是没有自由。
t_en: Wonderful... It's been so long since I had a normal conversation with someone. I don't need anything here, as they'll get me whatever I want... I'm just deprived of freedom.

t_ch: 他们做了什么？
t_en: What have they done?

t_ch: 一开始，他们伪装得很客气。他们拿各种东西诱惑我，想用物质和承诺交换我的知识…但没有效果。
t_en: At first, they pretended to be friendly with me. They tempted me with valuables and promises in return for my knowledge... all to no avail.

t_ch: 后来他们失去了耐心。他们开始逼迫我阅读和虚数应用理论相关的文献……
t_en: Eventually, they lost their patience and started forcing me to read articles related to the theory of imaginary application...

t_ch: …但「阻断药」的效果远比想象中好。它不止屏蔽了我脑海中的知识，甚至还阻断了我学习相关知识的能力。
t_en: ...But the drug was surprisingly effective. Not only did it block my access to the related knowledge in my mind, but it also hampered my ability to learn the knowledge.

t_ch: 再后来，他们接受了我已变成一个无用老人的事实。自某个时刻开始，他们的策略变成了静候我的死亡。
t_en: Much later on, they accepted the fact that I had been reduced to a useless old man. Starting from some point in time, they changed their strategy and decided to just wait for my death.

t_ch: 我很抱歉。
t_en: I'm sorry to hear that.

t_ch: …没有什么值得道歉的，这都是我理应付出的代价。
t_en: ...You don't have to. It's the price I have to pay.

t_ch: 被软禁的唯一好处，就是随着岁月和痛苦的累积，压在我身上的负罪感似乎减弱了一些。
t_en: The passing of time and the increasing pain seem to have alleviated my guilt, which is the only good thing about being confined here.

t_ch: 之后会发生什么？
t_en: What will happen next?

t_ch: 我从他们派来的人口中得到了一些零碎的消息。再加上我自己的推断…公司可能计划着等待我的肉体死亡后，对我大脑中残留的忆质做手脚。
t_en: I've gathered bits and pieces of info from the people they sent. Combined with my own deduction, I believe the IPC might be scheming to tamper with the memoria left in my brain after the death of my physical body.

t_ch: 他们大概认为，无论我用了何种手段缄口不言，一旦等到肉身消亡的一刻，余留下来的东西就只能任他们摆布。
t_en: They probably think that no matter how tight-lipped I am now, whatever remains of my dead body will be at their disposal.

t_ch: 我很害怕，年轻人…我害怕自己建立起来的防线会在死亡到来的一瞬完全崩塌。
t_en: I'm scared, youngster... I'm worried that all the precautions I've taken so far will be negated upon my demise.

t_ch: 如此发展下去，公司总有一天会再次掌握「虚数脉冲」的技术，并垄断它。到头来，我的一切努力都会变成徒……
t_en: At this rate, IPC will one day regain control of the Imaginary Pulse's technology and monopolize it. My efforts will all be in vain when the time comes...

t_ch: 请容我否认，博士——你的努力不是徒劳。
t_en: Please allow me to rebut that, doctor. Your effort isn't in vain.

t_ch: 我们又见面了，查德威克博士。
t_en: Here we are again, Dr. Chadwick.

t_ch: 螺丝…螺丝咕姆？！我没想到…我从来没想过自己还能再见到你……
t_en: Screw... Screwllum!? I never thought... I could see you again...

t_ch: 我也一度这么以为。命运无常，但它终于还是向你我展现了它善意的一面。
t_en: I once thought so too. Life is unpredictable, but it has eventually shown us its benevolent side.

t_ch: 我来是想告诉你，博士，你的努力没有白费。哪怕在肉身消亡以后，你仍然坚守着那些知识，没有给居心叵测之人留下可乘之机——
t_en: I'm here to tell you that your effort isn't in vain. Even after the death of your physical body, you're still safeguarding your knowledge, leaving no chance for those harboring ulterior motives...

t_ch: ——凭借的不是药物的效果，而是<color=#dbc291ff>铭刻于你意识根底的执念和坚持。</color>
t_en: It isn't the effect of the drug, but the <color=#dbc291ff>ingrained dedication and perseverance in your consciousness.</color>

t_ch: 那就好…那就再好不过了。
t_en: That's great... It couldn't be better.

t_ch: 你知道吗，螺丝？我一直都在后悔。第一次遇见你的时候，我屏蔽了自己的心之声。
t_en: You know what, Screwllum? I've always regretted not listening to the voice inside me when we first met.

t_ch: 一句简单的话，我却憋了大半辈子没能说出口。我想…现在是时候让我自己听见了。
t_en: It's just a simple phrase, but I haven't been able to say it. It's probably time I hear it myself now.

t_ch: 说出来吧，查德威克。人类的生命转瞬即逝，很少有人能不带着遗憾迈过终点。你的人生并不幸福——但能够拥有此刻，至少代表它是完整的。
t_en: Say it, Chadwick. Life is fleeting, and many reach the end with regrets. I can't say your life is a happy one, but it is at least complete when this moment takes place.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>我加入这项实验不是为了任何理念，只是为了我自己。我想让自己的才能和领域被世界看见，仅此而已。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>I joined the experiment not for a good cause, but for myself. I wanted my talents and expertise to be seen by the whole world, and that's it.</color>

t_ch: 谢谢你，螺丝咕姆。也谢谢你，孩子。我感觉…好多了，也许从未这么好过……
t_en: Thank you, Screwllum, and you. I feel... much better, probably never better...

t_ch: 这一路上辛苦你了，查德威克博士。现在，如果你不介意——就由我们来陪你走完这最后一段吧。
t_en: You've been through a lot, Dr. Chadwick. Now, if you don't mind, please let us be with you as you make your way to the end.

t_ch: 扶起查德威克博士。
t_en: Help Dr. Chadwick to his feet.

t_ch: 欢迎回来，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: Welcome back, {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 两个琥珀纪以后，查德威克博士终于获得了自由——他现在与我们同在。
t_en: Two Amber Eras later, Dr. Chadwick is finally freed. He is now with us.

t_ch: 他为什么会在梦中？
t_en: Why is he in the dream?

t_ch: 他怎么会变成这样？
t_en: How did he end up like this?

t_ch: 查德威克博士的梦魇成为了现实。他的肉身死去之时，公司用忆庭提供的先进技术提取了他大脑中的忆质，并将其封存在了忆泡之中。
t_en: What Dr. Chadwick dreaded the most became reality. After his demise, the IPC extracted the memoria in his brain with the advanced technology provided by the Garden of Recollection and sealed it in a Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 他们一定早有准备，所以整个忆质迁徙的过程十分迅速——迅速到连查德威克弥留之际的自我意识都被保存了下来……
t_en: They must have prepared for it in advance, as the process took place right after his death, so fast that even his consciousness in his final moments was preserved...

t_ch: …光是这样还不够。以那种方式被保存下来的忆质，说到底只是一滩「死水」罢了；要是在很长的时间里不被「搅动」，水里的东西迟早都会死光。
t_en: ...That step alone wasn't enough, as the memoria preserved that way was, in essence, a stagnant pool. If it remained inactive for too long a time, what was in the water would eventually die out.

t_ch: 所以，公司想到了办法——将查德威克送往匹诺康尼，通过联入梦境维持忆质的活性。推测：技术研发部一定设法与家族达成了某种协议。
t_en: Therefore, the IPC came up with a solution — sending Chadwick to Penacony to maintain the activity of the memoria. Speculation: The Technology Department must have struck some sort of a deal with The Family.

t_ch: 在家族的监视下，查德威克博士于梦中徘徊了两个琥珀纪。若不是你，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}——他们的监视还会一直延续。
t_en: Under The Family's supervision, Dr. Chadwick wandered in the dreamscape for two Amber Eras. If it weren't for you, {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}, their supervision would continue.

t_ch: 那现在呢？
t_en: What about now?

t_ch: 我想，查德威克博士该休息了。
t_en: I think it's time for Dr. Chadwick to rest.

t_ch: 现在他可以休息了。
t_en: Now, he can rest.

t_ch: 我同意，{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: I agree, {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 真是个奇迹。就靠着忆质里残留的那一丝丝自我，他居然能跟公司抗衡到现在…我当年果真没看错人。
t_en: This is such a miracle. It's hard to believe he has managed to resist the IPC until now with what little consciousness remaining in his memoria... I was right about him back then.

t_ch: 他一直都是个天才，黑塔女士——但他的意志并非一直如此坚韧。
t_en: He's always been a genius, Madam Herta, but he isn't always this determined.

t_ch: 科学和技术本身并无善恶观念。过去的几百年里，他一直在为自己年轻时的一个念头「赎罪」。
t_en: Science and technology is not good or evil. For the past hundreds of years, he's been trying to redeem himself for a thought he had when he was young.

t_ch: 当时听{NICKNAME}小鬼提到他的下落，我还以为终于有机会能接触到他的研究了…可惜，没想到最后事情竟会如此发展。
t_en: When {NICKNAME} told me about him, I thought I would finally have the chance to learn about his research... It's a pity that things turned out this way.

t_ch: 那些装置能够暂时维持忆泡的活性。只要关上它们，查德威克博士就能安静地睡去。
t_en: Those devices are used to maintain the vitality of the Memory Bubble temporarily. By turning them off, Dr. Chadwick will be able to enter a peaceful slumber.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，我们一起送它最后一程吧。
t_en: {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}, let's pay our final respects to him together.

t_ch: 在你眼前的不止是一台冰冷的装置，也是卡尔德隆•查德威克三分之一的生命。
t_en: Before you lies more than just a lifeless machine, it embodies a third of Calderon Chadwick's existence.

t_ch: 关闭维生装置。
t_en: Deactivate the life-sustaining system.

t_ch: 你本期待着世界会对这位天才的逝去做出回应……
t_en: You hoped for a response from the world to the departure of this brilliant mind...

t_ch: …但此刻荡漾于空气中的唯有沉默。
t_en: ...Yet, all that lingers is a profound silence in the air.

t_ch: 我对你的了解并不深刻，博士。相识百年，你我见面不过寥寥三次。
t_en: I don't know you very well, Doctor. We've known each other for a century, yet we've only met three times.

t_ch: 但还是请允许我称你一声——「朋友」。
t_en: Nevertheless, please allow me to address you as a friend.

t_ch: 关闭维生装置。
t_en: Deactivate the life-sustaining system.

t_ch: 这样说也许算不上慰藉：但在我的计算中，你的研究本有潜力拯救数以亿计的生灵。
t_en: I understand this may not be much of a consolation, but in my calculations, your research had the potential to save billions of lives.

t_ch: 可惜时代已随风去。在真正的灾难降临之前，我们只能祈祷——祈祷博识尊的视线能够投向下一位卡尔德隆•查德威克。
t_en: Regrettably, the era has slipped through our fingers like sand. Prior to the onset of an actual disaster, all we can do is pray — pray that the discerning gaze of Nous may turn toward the next Calderon Chadwick.

t_ch: 唔…我是不是也该说点什么？
t_en: Well... should I also say something?

t_ch: 咳…查德威克博士，我虽然没见过你本人——但我对你的头脑和研究深感钦佩。
t_en: *cough*... Dr. Chadwick, although I've never met you in person, I deeply admire your intellect and research.

t_ch: 你知道，我可不经常夸人…但咱们俩研究的领域有很多重合的地方。你发表的虚数应用理论不止一次给过我启发，甚至有点让我…唔…羡慕。
t_en: You know, I don't usually give compliments... But our fields of study share many commonalities. Your work on the theory of imaginary applications has sparked my inspiration on more than one occasion, and I have to say, it's even made me... umm... a tad jealous.

t_ch: 可惜咱们没机会一起共事了。听螺丝和那个小鬼说的，你这辈子可真是遭了不少罪——那就睡个好觉吧，博士。
t_en: It's a shame we never had the chance to work together. From what I've heard from Screwy and that troublemaker, you've had your share of hardships in your lifetime. Rest in peace, Doc.

t_ch: 关闭维生装置。
t_en: Deactivate the life-sustaining system.
Phew...

t_ch: 这样…就算完了？
t_en: Is this... over?

t_ch: 嗯，一个平静的结局。对于晚年的查德威克博士来说，算得上一种巨大的奢望。
t_en: Well, it's a calm conclusion. For Dr. Chadwick in his later years, This would count as quite a significant luxury.

t_ch: 没想到我居然会亲手为一位俱乐部的同僚送行。这感觉…还挺奇妙的。
t_en: I never imagined I'd be bidding farewell to a fellow Society member in person. It feels... rather peculiar.

t_ch: 真遗憾，要是我能早两个琥珀纪意识到——
t_en: It's a real pity. If only I had realized two Amber Eras earlier—

t_ch: 黑塔…女士……
t_en: Madam... Herta...

t_ch: 黑…塔…女…士……
t_en: Madam... Her... ta...

t_ch: …谁？是谁？！你们、你们刚才听见那个声音了吗？
t_en: ...Who? Who is it!? D—Did you all hear that voice just now?

t_ch: 空间站闹鬼了？！
t_en: Is the space station haunted!?

t_ch: 奇迹！是奇迹！
t_en: A miracle! It's a miracle!

t_ch: 是你在恶作剧吗，螺丝？！
t_en: Are you playing a prank, Screwllum!?

t_ch: 冷静下来，二位。我也听到了那个声音……
t_en: Calm down, both of you. I heard that voice too...

t_ch: 那难道是…查德威克博士残留的意识体？
t_en: Could it be... the residual consciousness of Dr. Chadwick?

t_ch: 黑…塔……
t_en: Her... ta...

t_ch: 请…靠近…些……
t_en: Please... come... closer...

t_ch: 又来了！不过这次的音量…似乎…减弱了一些。
t_en: It's happening again! But this time, it seems... quieter.

t_ch: 推测：查德威克博士的自我意识仍有微弱残留。它正在迫切尝试与我们对话。
t_en: Speculation: Dr. Chadwick's self-awareness still lingers faintly. It's making an eager effort to communicate with us.

t_ch: 黑塔女士，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}——我们靠近些吧。
t_en: Madam Herta, {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}, let's get closer.

t_ch: 黑塔…女士……
t_en: Madam... Herta...

t_ch: 我在听着呢，博士。你还有什么想说的，就趁现在吧。
t_en: I'm listening, doctor. If there's anything else you want to say, now's the time.

t_ch: 我很…荣幸……
t_en: I'm... honored...

t_ch: 我的…研究…我的…知识……
t_en: My... research... my... knowledge...

t_ch: 我会…留下……
t_en: I will... leave behind...

t_ch: 请你…接受……
t_en: Please... accept...

t_ch: …等等，你的意思是……
t_en: ...Wait, are you saying...

t_ch: 忆质…正在…消散……
t_en: Memoria... is... dissipating...

t_ch: 请你…靠近……
t_en: Please... come closer...

t_ch: 额头…触碰……
t_en: Touch... foreheads...

t_ch: …我明白了，博士。
t_en: ...I understand, doctor.

t_ch: 黑塔的人偶向前迈进了一步，用额头抵住了颤抖的忆泡。残驻着查德威克意识的忆泡瞬间破裂…数秒之后，黑塔睁开了眼睛。
t_en: Herta's puppet takes a step forward, pressing her forehead against the trembling Memory Bubble. The Memory Bubble, which still holds traces of Chadwick's consciousness, ruptures in an instant... and a few seconds later, Herta opens her eyes.

t_ch: 卡尔德隆•查德威克……
t_en: Calderon Chadwick...

t_ch: 真让人火大啊…明明只是个只有几十年寿命的普通人，居然能取得这么令人瞠目结舌的成就。
t_en: It's absolutely infuriating... He was nothing more than an ordinary person with a lifespan of just a few decades, yet he managed to achieve such astonishing feats.

t_ch: 他对你说什么了？
t_en: What did he say to you?

t_ch: …所有。就在刚刚那一瞬间，他把自己毕生的成果都托付给了我。
t_en: ...Everything. In that brief moment, he entrusted all his life's work to me.

t_ch: 看来你真的很欣赏他。
t_en: It seems like you truly admire him.

t_ch: 欣赏？可以这么说吧。不过老实说…我是有些羡慕，还有点…妒忌。
t_en: Admire? I suppose you could say that. But to be honest... I'm somewhat envious, and a bit... jealous.

t_ch: 就在刚刚那一瞬间，他把自己毕生的成果都托付给了我。
t_en: At that very moment, he entrusted me with all his life's work.

t_ch: 看来在生命的最后时刻，卡尔德隆•查德威克选择了相信你，黑塔女士——哪怕你们素未谋面。
t_en: It appears that in the final moments of his life, Calderon Chadwick chose to place his trust in you, Madam Herta — even though the two of you had never met before.

t_ch: 有机生命的理性，着实令人难以捉摸。但有机生命的冲动，往往蕴含壮阔且浪漫的诗意——令我震撼。
t_en: The reasoning behind organic life is truly unpredictable. Nevertheless, the impulses of organic life frequently embody a grand and romantic poetry that never fails to leave me in awe.

t_ch: …了不起，太了不起了。上万条公式和方程此刻正在我脑海里闪现…我从没想过还有这种剖析问题的方式。
t_en: ...Remarkable, truly remarkable. Tens of thousands of formulas and equations are flashing through my mind right now... I never imagined there could be such a way to analyze the problem.

t_ch: 从虚数流溢到虚数坍缩…从理论到应用…从一纸论文到「虚数脉冲」的问世……
t_en: From Imaginary overflow to Imaginary implosion... from theory to application... from a mere paper to the birth of the Imaginary Pulse...

t_ch: 您看到什么了，黑塔女士？
t_en: What did you see, Madam Herta?

t_ch: 我看到了…他最后留下的一段话。
t_en: I saw... the final words he left behind.

t_ch: 「致黑塔女士：尽管我在有生之年守住了自己的阵地，但以公司掌握的资源，他们终有一日会找到复现『虚数坍缩脉冲』的技术和方法。」
t_en: "To Madam Herta: While I've held my position during my lifetime, given the resources the IPC possesses, they will inevitably discover the means and techniques to reproduce the Imaginary Implosion Pulse."

t_ch: 「木已成舟，自『虚数脉冲』问世的一刻起，宇宙的任何一隅都注定要警惕来自它的威胁。我无力令时光倒流，但我至少还能做到一件事——」
t_en: "The die was cast at the moment the Imaginary Pulse was created. Every corner of the universe is destined to be on guard against its threats. I can't turn back time, but there's still one thing I can do..."

t_ch: 「——将我掌握的知识和技术交给值得托付的人，为建立足以制衡公司势力的『威慑体系』打下基础。」
t_en: "...I can entrust my knowledge and technology to those who are worthy, thereby establishing the groundwork for a deterrence system robust enough to counterbalance the IPC's might."

t_ch: 「我的生命已经走到了终点。但从这一刻起，我想要成为一柄利剑，永远悬在霸权和枭恶的头顶。」
t_en: "My life is nearing its conclusion. Yet, from this moment forward, I aspire to be an unyielding blade eternally poised above tyranny and malevolence."

t_ch: 壁炉取暖开着，但整个客房如同冰窖一样寒冷。
t_en: The fireplace heating is turned on, yet the whole room is chilly like an icy cavern.

t_ch: 你无法想象一个正常人能长期生活在如此之低的室温下。
t_en: You can't imagine a normal person living in a space so cold.

t_ch: 你掂了掂这只已经落灰的旅行箱——它显然空空如也。
t_en: You weigh a dusty suitcase with your hand — it is obviously empty.

t_ch: 它也许已经在这个房间里静躺了上百年的时光。
t_en: It may have been left untouched in this room for centuries.

t_ch: 在这间死气沉沉的客房里，唯一能提醒你时间仍在流动的便是入梦池内那些呼吸着的、窜动着的液泡。
t_en: In this dingy, stuffy room, the only thing that reminds you of flowing time is the pulsing, hovering liquid bubbles in the Dreampool.

t_ch: 但它们也不会告诉你查德威克博士身在何处。
t_en: But they won't be able to tell you where Professor Chadwick went.

t_ch: 我会尝试和家族的高层斡旋，争取能将查德威克的忆泡带离匹诺康尼。
t_en: I'll negotiate with the upper echelons of The Family toward the aim of taking Chadwick's Memory Bubble with me.

t_ch: 在那之前，希望你能从查德威克那里了解到事件的全貌——也包括在他失联之后发生的事。
t_en: Until then, I hope you'll learn the full story from Chadwick, including matters that happened before he went missing.

t_ch: 祝你好梦，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: May you have a nice dream, {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 莫伊拉直直地盯着你，你从她眼中看到了恼怒和无奈。
t_en: Moira stares daggers at you, and you can infer both angst and resignation in her eyes.

t_ch: 如果螺丝咕姆不在身边，你觉得她有可能当场把你撕成碎片。
t_en: If Screwllum wasn't around, you guess she might just try to tear you apart right here and now.

t_ch: 这里究竟是什么地方，杜？
t_en: What exactly is this place, Dud?

t_ch: 很遗憾，我不能告诉你这颗星球的具体坐标——上面的命令。这是为了防止你们…唔…半途而废，希望你能理解，查。
t_en: Forgive me, but I cannot tell you the exact coordinates of this planet — orders from up top. It's to prevent you all from... err, abandoning the task beforehand. I hope you'll understand, Chad.

t_ch: 但我可以告诉你的是：你在这里绝对不会被打扰。
t_en: But I can tell you this: You will absolutely not be disturbed here.

t_ch: 啊…原来如此。但你应该也明白吧？只要我想，我肯定有办法搞清楚自己在哪。这不是什么难事。
t_en: Ah... I see, but you should know too? That if I wanted, I would find a way to locate my position. It's not that hard.

t_ch: 杜德利发出了笑声，没有正面回应。他当然明白，毕竟站在他身边的是一位「天才」。
t_en: Dudley laughs out loud instead of fielding a response. Of course he knows that: It is a genius who stands before him.

t_ch: 动作快！把敢反抗的人都抓起来！
t_en: Hurry it up! Round up anyone who dares to resist!

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>任何人都不得耽误起爆！</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>No one should hinder the detonation!</color>

t_ch: 他们…他们真的打算使用「脉冲」吗？！
t_en: They... Are they really going to use the Pulse!?

t_ch: 查德威克博士…查德威克博士在哪？只有他、只有他能阻止这一切！
t_en: Professor Chadwick... Where is he? Only he can put a stop to all this!

t_ch: 长官！我们找到卡尔德隆•查德威克博士了！
t_en: Captain! We've located Professor Calderon Chadwick!

t_ch: 好，时间还来得及——把他带到控制室来，我去通知杜德利总监。动作快！
t_en: Good, we still have time then. Take him to the control room. I'll notify Director Dudley. Hurry, go!

t_ch: 几本书籍散落在地上。你定睛看了看书名，确信这些书本与查德威克的专业和研究课题毫不相干。
t_en: A few books are scattered on the floor. You peer at their names, and are certain they have nothing to do with Chadwick's specialization nor research subjects.

t_ch: 你很难想象那位老人会对《仙舟持明族民乐考》感兴趣，或许他真的已经在这里待了太久了。
t_en: You find it hard to believe that old man would be interested in Xianzhou Vidyadhara Folk Music Theory — or perhaps he must have lived here for too long.

t_ch: 老人面前的书桌上叠放着几封未寄出的信件。
t_en: The desk before the old man is stacked with unsent letters.

t_ch: 信件的封面上用潦草的字体书写着：「寄出不予批准，建议退回」。
t_en: On a letter cover, written in hasty handwriting: "Unauthorized send request. Return recommended."

t_ch: 墙壁上的保险箱，不知道里面保存着什么。
t_en: One wonders what's inside the safe on the wall.

t_ch: 也有可能是完全空的…因为此处的卡尔德隆•查德威克不可能再私藏秘密。
t_en: It could be completely empty... because the Calderon Chadwick right here has no more secrets he can hide.

t_ch: （查德威克博士，是被软禁在了这里吗？）
t_en: (Professor Chadwick has been placed under house arrest here?)

t_ch: （我该去和他聊聊。）
t_en: (I should go talk to him.)

t_ch: 在我的计算中，卡尔德隆•查德威克的研究本能拯救数以亿计的生灵——现在看来，这一计算结果有极大概率将会应验。
t_en: According to my calculations, Calderon Chadwick's research could have saved hundreds of millions of lives. Now, it appears that this outcome has an extremely high rate of becoming reality.

t_ch: 愿你能在那片树海中找到永恒的安宁，博士。
t_en: May you find eternal peace among that sea of trees, doctor.

t_ch: 没想到他居然会把毕生的成果都托付给我…他是不知道这座空间站跟公司之间还有合作关系吗？
t_en: To think he would entrust his life's work to me... Did he not know this space station was connected with the IPC?

t_ch: 但是他留下的愿望…果然还是得尊重才行。
t_en: But his wishes... They must be honored.

t_ch: 那就想办法绕过公司的眼线吧。赌上「天才」之名，我一定会为卡尔德隆•查德威克博士办到这件事。
t_en: Find a way to circumvent the IPC's surveillance then. With my reputation as a Genius on the line, I will surely get this done for Dr. Calderon Chadwick.

t_ch: （刚刚我所经历的，就是查德威克的前半生吗？）
t_en: (What I just experienced, was that the first half of Chadwick's life?)

t_ch: （但这段回忆还没结束…去看看接下来在他身上发生了什么吧。）
t_en: (But this memory isn't over yet... Let's take a look at what happened to him after.)

t_ch: 对于「梦」，我向来抱怀敬畏，因而不常光顾匹诺康尼那所谓的「盛会之梦」。
t_en: I've always regarded dreams with awe, hence I rarely frequent Penacony, that Planet of Festivities.

t_ch: 与我而言，所谓的美梦呈现出来的样貌甜腻且虚假，与「梦」原本应有的模样相去甚远。
t_en: To me, the so-called sweet dreams are often saccharine and insincere, a stark contrast to the true shape of a dream.

t_ch: 不知在梦境中沉浸许久以后，还留在现实中的查德威克是否安好。
t_en: I wonder, after immersing himself in the dreamscape for so long, is Dr. Chadwick doing well in the realm of reality?

t_ch: （奇怪…这房间完全没有宾客入住的迹象。）
t_en: (Strange... There are no signs of any guests having stayed in this room.)

t_ch: （先四下看看吧…）
t_en: (Let's take a look around...)

t_ch: 非常整洁的房间…
t_en: A very tidy room...

t_ch: 没有入住的痕迹。
t_en: No signs of occupancy.

t_ch: 或许可以先四下看看。
t_en: Perhaps we should look around first.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，我想…我大概知道查德威克在哪了。
t_en: {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}, I think... I might know where Chadwick is.

t_ch: 请过来看看。
t_en: Please come and take a look.

t_ch: 螺丝先生，请告诉我…俱乐部的各位是怎么看待我的？
t_en: Mr. Screwllum, please tell me... How do the members of the Society view me?

t_ch: 我并未就此收集过多数人的意见。不过，有一位女士的评价，我想我可以代为转述。
t_en: I have not gathered too many opinions on this matter. However, there is one lady's assessment that I believe I can convey on her behalf.

t_ch: 「卡尔德隆•查德威克用短短数年的时间，突破了很多长生的老妖怪几辈子都突破不了的难题。他才是那个货真价实的『天才』。」
t_en: "In a few short years, Calderon Chadwick achieved breakthroughs that even ancient immortals couldn't conquer. He is a genuine genius."

t_ch: 你听到查德威克发出一声满意的笑…然后不再说话。
t_en: You hear Chadwick emit a satisfied laugh... and then he no longer speaks.

t_ch: 带我们醒来吧，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
t_en: Let's wake up, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 那只蓝色羽毛的孩子，它还没学会找到回家的路，就在捉迷藏时走丢了……
t_en: That blue-feathered child... it already got lost playing hide-and-seek before it learned how to find the way back home...

t_ch: 拜托了，客人！请你帮忙去找找那孩子吧，啾！
t_en: Please, guest! Help look for that child, tweet!

t_ch: 关于你自己…
t_en: About yourself...

t_ch: 鸟，折纸小鸟，或者「湛蓝爵士」。只要别给我添乱，想怎么叫都行。
t_en: Bird, origami bird, or Dr. Blues. You can call me anything you want as long as you don't give me any trouble.

t_ch: 托那位「钟表匠」的福，我正在忙着修这座流梦礁——哦，不对，应该说「钟表小子」让我建的「清醒村」。
t_en: It's thanks to that Watchmaker that I'm busy fixing up this Dreamflux Reef. Oh, correction — I should say it's thanks to Clockie that I'm building Rouseville.

t_ch: 咳，看过《钟表小子》动画片吗？按设定来说，「折纸小鸟只认识钟表小子」。
t_en: Ahem, have you seen Clockie's cartoons? According to the setting, origami birds only know Clockie.

t_ch: 但你说话不像折纸小鸟。
t_en: But you don't talk like an origami bird.

t_ch: 呃，为什么？
t_en: Err, why's that?

t_ch: 你知道「钟表匠」。
t_en: You know the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 那当然，这里所有人都知道「钟表匠」…但只有很少一部分人知道他真正想要什么。
t_en: Of course, everyone knows the Watchmaker around here... But only a handful know what he really wants.

t_ch: 行了，比起在这跟我说废话，你更该去找找那家伙留下的东西。
t_en: All right, rather than talking about the pointless, you'd be better off looking for the stuff that fella left behind.

t_ch: 你不只是个动画角色。
t_en: You're not just an animated character.

t_ch: 好眼力。可惜，暂时还没到解密「折纸小鸟」的环节。
t_en: Good eye. Unfortunately, it's not time yet to solve the "Origami Bird" arc.

t_ch: 比起在这跟我兜圈子，你更该去找找那位「钟表匠」留下的东西。
t_en: Instead of going around in circles with me, you should look for the stuff that the Watchmaker left behind.

t_ch: 你不会啾啾叫！
t_en: You don't tweet!

t_ch: …啾。满意了？
t_en: ...Tweet. Satisfied now?

t_ch: 一个两个的，怎么都在纠结这种幼稚的设定…比起在这跟我兜圈子，你更该去找找那位「钟表匠」留下的东西。
t_en: What's with you people and your obsession with such a juvenile setting... Instead of going around in circles with me, you should look for the stuff that the Watchmaker left behind.

t_ch: 让我看看…我这集的「戏份」是督促美梦的来客，去寻找「小镇背后的秘密」。
t_en: Let's see... My role in this episode is to urge Dreamville's visitors to search for "the town's hidden secret."

t_ch: 听到了吗？快去吧——这座梦境，没你想得那么简单。
t_en: Did you hear me? Quickly now. This Dreamscape isn't as simple as you think.

t_ch: 嗯？这顶帽子…看来你已经找到「钟表匠」留下的「{RUBY_B#遗产}宝藏{RUBY_E#}」了。
t_en: Hmm? This hat... Looks like you've found the {RUBY_B#Legacy}treasure{RUBY_E#} that the Watchmaker left behind.

t_ch: 这么说，他的心愿完成了？
t_en: Does this mean that his last wishes are fulfilled?

t_ch: 你向湛蓝爵士转述了在匹诺康尼发生的一切——关于「秩序」的阴谋，以及「钟表匠」的遗愿。
t_en: You tell Dr. Blues about all that has happened in Penacony — about the Order's conspiracy, and the last wishes of the Watchmaker.
Hmph...

t_ch: 「钟表匠」这家伙也是死脑筋…设计这么大一盘局，把胜算全押在别人身上。兜兜转转一大圈，没想到最后真给他找到了「完美结局」。
t_en: This Watchmaker sure is a stubborn one... planning such a gigantic plot and betting it all on someone else. I never thought he'd actually find the perfect ending after going around in such a huge circle.

t_ch: 好了，这下「钟表匠」的心愿圆满达成，那只贪杯的猎犬估计也光荣退休了——
t_en: All right, now the Watchmaker's wishes have been fulfilled. That drunken hound can also probably retire with honor...

t_ch: …这个地方，又只剩我一只鸟了。
t_en: ...And I'm the only bird left in this place again.

t_ch: 你不打算离开这里吗？
t_en: You don't intend to leave?

t_ch: 我？我是折纸小鸟，懂吗？只能出现在梦里的那种。
t_en: Me? I'm an origami bird, don't you get it? The sort that can only appear in dreams.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼容不了一只不说「啾」的鸟，于是我就听了那个「钟表匠」的忽悠，在这儿建了个新巢。后来又有一些爱唱反调的人也被他拽了过来，这儿就成了流梦礁。
t_en: Penacony does not welcome birds that can't tweet, which is why I trusted the Watchmaker's deception and built a new nest here. Later, he dragged a bunch of naysayers here, and this place became Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: ——从某种意义上来说，这里是个巨大号的「避难所」，专门接收那些不为梦境所容，却又离不开梦的可怜虫。
t_en: ...To a certain extent, this is a grand haven, specifically sheltering poor souls who could neither fit into the Dreamscape, nor leave the dream.

t_ch: 是你建造了流梦礁？
t_en: You built Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 鸟也能当筑梦师？
t_en: Birds can be Dreamweavers too?

t_ch: 没错。好好珍惜吧，匹诺康尼可再难有我这么优秀的筑梦师了，我——
t_en: That's right. Treasure all this, for Penacony will be hard-pressed to produce another Dreamweaver as gifted as me. I...

t_ch: 你面前的小鸟突然哑火。它沉默了两秒，态度变得极为冷淡。
t_en: The bird before you suddenly goes mute. It is silent for a couple of seconds, and its demeanor turns aloof.

t_ch: 算了，跟你没关系。
t_en: Forget it, it's none of your business.

t_ch: 我忙得很，去找别的倒霉鬼当导游吧。慢走不送。
t_en: I'm very busy. Go find another unlucky soul to be your guide. Don't let me keep you.

t_ch: …嘀嗒！{NICKNAME}，快过来！
t_en: ...Tick-tock! Get over here quick, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 滴答，湛蓝爵士不对劲！
t_en: Tick-tock, something's wrong with Dr. Blues!

t_ch: 它和我在美梦小镇里见到的样子完全不同，它的身上缺了很多折纸小鸟该有的东西！
t_en: It's completely different from when I saw it in Dreamville. It's missing something that an origami bird should have!

t_ch: 不仅如此，那副折纸的身体下好像装着另一个灵魂……湛蓝爵士，或许它不是普通的折纸小鸟？
t_en: Not just that, it seems like there's another soul inside that paper body... Maybe Dr. Blues is no ordinary Origami Bird?

t_ch: 有点道理。
t_en: You've got a point.

t_ch: 还不好说…
t_en: It's hard to say...

t_ch: 我早就想到了！
t_en: I knew it!

t_ch: 滴答…要想寻找湛蓝爵士的过去，就得从它提供的信息入手——能够建造出流梦礁的家伙，肯定不是无名之鸟！
t_en: Tick-tock... If we're to search for Dr. Blues' past, we'll just have to start with the information it provided. A bird capable of building Dreamflux Reef must not be an ordinary bird!

t_ch: 我们先去「筑梦师们聚集的地方」看看吧，滴答！
t_en: Let's take a look at the place where Dreamweavers gather first, tick-tock!

t_ch: 关于你自己…
t_en: About yourself...

t_ch: 鸟，折纸小鸟，或者「湛蓝爵士」。只要别给我添乱，想怎么叫都行。
t_en: Bird, origami bird, or Dr. Blues. You can call me anything you want as long as you don't give me any trouble.

t_ch: 除了寻找小鸟的事儿以外一概不回复——我忙得很，要找导游麻烦别人去。
t_en: I'm not answering anything else other than how to find the birds. I'm very busy, please find someone else to be your guide.

t_ch: ——即使在梦境中，也绝不能松懈！
t_en: ...No time to relax, not even in a Dreamscape!

t_ch: 总有些学生，走出折纸大学后就把课上的内容抛在脑后…筑梦学以建筑为技术基底，既然如此，安全与合理就是最关键的问题。
t_en: There are always students who forget what they learned in Paperfold University after graduating... Dreamweaving is based on architecture as its technical foundation, so safety and practicality are the utmost concerns.

t_ch: 您应该重新看看那几个新方案。它们完全违背了建筑设计的准则！
t_en: You should look at those new plans again. They completely defy the principles of architectural design!

t_ch: 呵呵，教授，我理解你的担忧，但我们也应尊重筑梦师们的自我表达。
t_en: Heh-heh, I understand your frustration, Professor, but we should also respect the Dreamweavers' freedom of expression.

t_ch: 请您不要避重就轻，<color=#dbc291ff>家主</color>——这可是有关于未来筑梦教育的大事！
t_en: Please refrain from being dismissive, <color=#dbc291ff>esteemed head</color>! This concerns the cornerstone of future Dreamweaving education!

t_ch: 当然，我会再次考虑的。不过…今天似乎还有别的客人来访。
t_en: Of course, I'll reconsider. But... we appear to have other guests today.

t_ch: …哼，我会再提醒您的。哎，现在的筑梦师已经是一代不如一代了……
t_en: ...Hmph, I'll be sure to remind you. Bah, the current generation of Dreamweavers is always more lacking than the last...

t_ch: 阿锡卡叹了口气，转身离开了……
t_en: Asika sighs, then turns and leaves...

t_ch: 晚上好，逐梦客{F#小姐}{M#先生}。找我有什么事？
t_en: Good evening, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Dreamchaser. How can I help you?

t_ch: 家主大人？
t_en: Esteemed head?

t_ch: 只是一个称呼，不必在意——权力与地位会让人盲目，在非工作时间，我更希望别人能称呼我为「惠特克爵士」。
t_en: Please dispense with the formalities. Authority and power blind the soul. When it's after work hours, I much prefer people address me as Sir Whittaker.

t_ch: 还有其他问题吗？
t_en: Do you have any other questions?

t_ch: 我也想学筑梦。
t_en: I wish to learn how to weave dreams too.

t_ch: 当然可以，家族在「太阳的时刻」设立了折纸大学，等到开课之日，我一定会欢迎你来旁听筑梦课程的。
t_en: Of course you may. The Family has established the Paperfold University in the Moment of Sol. When classes start, I'll be sure to welcome you to sit in on our dreamweaving course.

t_ch: 还有其他问题吗？
t_en: Do you have any other questions?

t_ch: 鸟能当筑梦师吗？
t_en: Can birds be Dreamweavers?

t_ch: 鸟…呵呵，你是说那位「钟表匠」创作的动画片吧。
t_en: Birds... Heh-heh, you mean the cartoons that the Watchmaker created.

t_ch: 那真是个不错的比喻，这片梦境中的每一位筑梦师确实都是「鸟」。我们「隐夜鸫」的符号，不就是在夜晚歌唱的鸟儿吗？
t_en: It's not a bad metaphor at all. The Dreamweavers in this Dreamscape are really all birds. Our Nightingale symbol — is it not a bird that sings at night?

t_ch: 在这片梦境中，鸟本就是筑梦师的象征。这个意象表现在了各种事物中，也在一些文艺作品里有所展现。
t_en: In this Dreamscape, birds are a symbol of Dreamweavers. This sentiment has been expressed in objects of all sorts, and shown up in some pieces of artistry.

t_ch: 比如折纸小鸟？
t_en: Such as origami birds?

t_ch: 没错。艺术与现实总是相辅相成——据我所知，《钟表小子》动画中的折纸小鸟正代表了曾经为建造梦境奉献一切的筑梦师们。
t_en: That's right. Art and reality always complement each other. According to what I know, the origami birds in Clockie's cartoons symbolize the Dreamweavers that gave their all to build the Dreamscapes.

t_ch: 可惜，匹诺康尼的宾客与逐梦客很少有人对历史感兴趣…如果你需要，我这里还保留着关于那个年代的筑梦故事。
t_en: Alas, few guests and Dreamchasers in Penacony show an interest in history... If you're interested, I've got some dreamweaving stories of that era kept here.

t_ch: 一张老照片从资料的折页中滑落。相纸看着有些年头，泛黄的纸上印着四个年轻的身影。
t_en: An old photograph slips out from one of the folded pages of the file stack. It looks dated, with four young figures printed on its faded yellow background.

t_ch: 这四人或许就是资料中提到的「折纸筑梦师」。
t_en: These four may just be the Origami Dreamweavers mentioned in the file.

t_ch: 找到想要的信息了吗？
t_en: Found the info you wanted?

t_ch: 关于这张照片…
t_en: About this photo...

t_ch: 哦…真是张令人怀念的相片。
t_en: Oh... This photo really brings back memories.

t_ch: 它属于曾经的「折纸筑梦师」费歇尔，也是我的导师——他在晚年时带领我们这些折纸大学的学生一起整理了隐夜鸫的家史资料，这照片应该就是那时放进去的。
t_en: It once belonged to Origami Dreamweaver Fischer, who was also my mentor. In his later years, he led us Paperfold University students in compiling a family history of the Nightingales. This photo should have been from that time.

t_ch: 关于「折纸筑梦师」…
t_en: About the Origami Dreamweavers...

t_ch: 他们是「最初的筑梦师」格拉克斯教授的学徒，更是创造十二时刻的传奇筑梦师。如今你目之可及的每一片梦境都留下了他们的手笔。
t_en: They were students of the first Dreamweaver, Professor Glaux, and legendary Dreamweavers who created the Twelve Hours. What you witness in each Dreamscape is their legacy.

t_ch: …只可惜，这片梦境只铭记了他们的作品，而他们的名字却被慢慢遗忘。
t_en: ...Alas, it's a shame that this Dreamscape only has their works engraved, but left their names long forgotten.

t_ch: 关于逐梦年代…
t_en: About the Dreamchaser era...

t_ch: 那是一个无数梦想萌发的时代。费歇尔先生曾跟我讲述过那个年代——
t_en: That was a time when countless dreams sprouted. Mr. Fischer once told me of that age...

t_ch: 当时的匹诺康尼刚刚完成对梦境的开辟，各大家系也还未完全投入同谐的怀抱，无数机遇潜藏在梦中待人挖掘，因此无数逐梦客蜂拥而至，渴望财富与成功。
t_en: During that time, Penacony had only just completed the Dreamscape's development. The lineages weren't completely given unto Harmony's embrace yet, and countless opportunities buried in dreams were waiting to be unearthed, resulting in numerous Dreamchasers gathering like moths to a flame, thirsting for fortune and success.

t_ch: 为建成真正的盛会之星，担任隐夜鸫家主的格拉克斯教授开始向全宇宙募集最优秀的人才，带领他们探索改造梦境的方法——也就是筑梦技术。
t_en: To build a true Planet of Festivities, Professor Glaux — who was the Nightingale's family head — began recruiting the most stellar talents across the cosmos, leading them in the exploration of methods to modify the Dreamscape — what you know as dreamweaving technology.

t_ch: 他最优秀的四个学生被赋予了「折纸筑梦师」的称号。他们是建筑师，更是征服梦境的伟大逐梦客。
t_en: His four most outstanding students were coined the Origami Dreamweavers. They were architects, but first and foremost, they were great Dreamchasers who conquered the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 问得差不多了。
t_en: I'm about done with my questions.

t_ch: 虽然只是我的猜测——钟表小子动画中那几位小鸟头领的原型，正是这几位「折纸筑梦师」。
t_en: This may just be my speculation, but the original inspiration for those leader-birds in the Clockie cartoons — they were these Origami Dreamweavers.

t_ch: 等下！数量对不上！
t_en: Hang on! The numbers don't add up!

t_ch: （折纸小鸟共有五种颜色…但这里的传奇筑梦师只有四位！到底谁才是「湛蓝爵士」？）
t_en: (Origami birds come in five colors... but there are only four legendary Dreamweavers here! Which one is Dr. Blues exactly?)

t_ch: 这个嘛…我也并不清楚，如果你感兴趣，就请自己去探寻真相吧。
t_en: About this... I'm not too sure either. If you're interested, you may wish to seek out the truth yourself.

t_ch: 时间会让过往的故事消磨褪色，被纸醉金迷掩埋…年轻人，我很高兴你还能记得他们。
t_en: Time will cause the colors of past tales to fade, buried by extravagance and embellishment... Young one, I'm very glad that you still remember them.

t_ch: 这张照片就暂借给你吧。希望你能够探明这背后的往事。
t_en: I'll lend you this photograph for now. I hope you can unearth the history behind this matter.

t_ch: 你好，年轻的逐梦客，还有别的事情吗？
t_en: Hello, young Dreamchaser. You have something else to ask?

t_ch: 关于这张照片…
t_en: About this photo...

t_ch: 哦…真是张令人怀念的相片。
t_en: Oh... This photo really brings back memories.

t_ch: 它属于曾经的「折纸筑梦师」费歇尔，也是我的导师——他在晚年时带领我们这些折纸大学的学生一起整理了隐夜鸫的家史资料，这照片应该就是那时放进去的。
t_en: It once belonged to Origami Dreamweaver Fischer, who was also my mentor. In his later years, he led us Paperfold University students in compiling a family history of the Nightingales. This photo should have been from that time.

t_ch: 关于「折纸筑梦师」…
t_en: About the Origami Dreamweavers...

t_ch: 他们是「最初的筑梦师」格拉克斯教授的学徒，更是创造十二时刻的传奇筑梦师。如今你目之可及的每一片梦境都留下了他们的手笔。
t_en: They were students of the first Dreamweaver, Professor Glaux, and legendary Dreamweavers who created the Twelve Hours. What you witness in each Dreamscape is their legacy.

t_ch: …只可惜，这片梦境只铭记了他们的作品，而他们的名字却被慢慢遗忘。
t_en: ...Alas, it's a shame that this Dreamscape only has their works engraved, but left their names long forgotten.

t_ch: 关于逐梦年代…
t_en: About the Dreamchaser era...

t_ch: 那是一个无数梦想萌发的时代。费歇尔先生曾跟我讲述过那个年代——
t_en: That was a time when countless dreams sprouted. Mr. Fischer once told me of that age...

t_ch: 当时的匹诺康尼刚刚完成对梦境的开辟，各大家系也还未完全投入同谐的怀抱，无数机遇潜藏在梦中待人挖掘，因此无数逐梦客蜂拥而至，渴望财富与成功。
t_en: During that time, Penacony had only just completed the Dreamscape's development. The lineages weren't completely given unto Harmony's embrace yet, and countless opportunities buried in dreams were waiting to be unearthed, resulting in numerous Dreamchasers gathering like moths to a flame, thirsting for fortune and success.

t_ch: 为建成真正的盛会之星，担任隐夜鸫家主的格拉克斯教授开始向全宇宙募集最优秀的人才，带领他们探索改造梦境的方法——也就是筑梦技术。
t_en: To build a true Planet of Festivities, Professor Glaux — who was the Nightingale's family head — began recruiting the most stellar talents across the cosmos, leading them in the exploration of methods to modify the Dreamscape — what you know as dreamweaving technology.

t_ch: 他最优秀的四个学生被赋予了「折纸筑梦师」的称号。他们是建筑师，更是征服梦境的伟大逐梦客。
t_en: His four most outstanding students were coined the Origami Dreamweavers. They were architects, but first and foremost, they were great Dreamchasers who conquered the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 没什么要问的。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 时间会让过往的故事消磨褪色，被纸醉金迷掩埋…年轻人，我很高兴你还能记得他们。
t_en: Time will cause the colors of past tales to fade, buried by extravagance and embellishment... Young one, I'm very glad that you still remember them.

t_ch: 希望你能探明这背后的往事。
t_en: I hope you can unearth the history behind this matter.

t_ch: …有何贵干？
t_en: ...How may I help?

t_ch: 我理解了一切！
t_en: I understand everything!

t_ch: 你是隐夜鸫的筑梦师？
t_en: You are a Nightingale Dreamweaver?

t_ch: 我是谁跟你没关系。有这闲工夫不如去找找流梦礁的折纸小鸟。
t_en: Who I am is none of your business. If you've got time to spare, you might as well go look for Dreamflux Reef's Origami Birds.

t_ch: （向湛蓝爵士展示泛黄的相片）
t_en: (Show Dr. Blues the faded photo)

t_ch: 哪来的照片？都说了我忙得很，我——
t_en: Where'd you get this photograph from? I already told you I'm very busy, I...

t_ch: …这是……
t_en: ...This is...

t_ch: 小鸟愣住了，你第一次从它那张折纸的面孔上读出类似「茫然」的情绪。
t_en: The bird is dumbfounded. It is the first time you see the definition of "bewilderment" written all over that origami face.

t_ch: 这是我的照片？不对…又好像不一样……
t_en: This is my photo? No... It seems different...

t_ch: 上面没有你吗？
t_en: You're not in it?

t_ch: 这不是我的照片，但我认识，不，我很熟悉这几个人……
t_en: This isn't my photo, but I recognize, no, I'm very familiar with these few...

t_ch: 你认识这些筑梦师？
t_en: You know these Dreamweavers?

t_ch: 我应该认识他们，甚至说，很熟悉……
t_en: I should know them, or rather, I'm very familiar...

t_ch: 我还找到了一些过去的资料。
t_en: I've even found some information from the past.

t_ch: 《筑梦师档案记录》……
t_en: The Dreamweaver Archives...

t_ch: …这些的都是什么乱七八糟的！真正的隐夜鸫压根不是这样的，我们——
t_en: ...What's all this nonsense about! The true Nightingales weren't like this at all, we...

t_ch: 我们、我们……
t_en: W—We...

t_ch: 是什么样的？
t_en: What were you like?

t_ch: …我不记得了。
t_en: ...I don't remember.

t_ch: 糟糕，当鸟当久了，好像忘了什么很重要的事情……
t_en: Oh no, I've been a bird for so long. It's as if I've forgotten something important...

t_ch: 刚好，我来帮帮你吧！
t_en: Well, then, you should let me help you!

t_ch: 不要。我见过钟表小子那套玩意儿…让别人钻进自己的脑子？太恶心了，我不要。
t_en: No. I've seen what Clockie does... Letting someone into your brain? That's disgusting. No.
